<!-- Auto-generated Sarna-style mech reference pages. QKS data was treated as authoritative over spreadsheet-derived stats. -->

# Battlefield Roles

These role labels are editorial shorthand for the wiki. They are inferred from the technical profiles rather than treated as formal in-universe designations.

| Role | Typical Meaning |
|---|---|
| Scout | Light, fast machines used for reconnaissance, harassment, and pursuit. |
| Striker | Mobile attack mechs that rely on speed and focused firepower. |
| Cavalry Striker | Mobile machines with jump capability, useful for flanking and rapid repositioning. |
| Line Mech | General-purpose battlefield machines suited to standard formations. |
| Brawler | Heavier, slower mechs built to survive close or mid-range punishment. |
| Fire Support | Missile, rocket, or long-range platforms intended to support from behind the line. |
| Missile Skirmisher | Mobile missile or rocket platforms that trade sustained durability for harassment fire. |
| Assault | Heavy front-line machines designed to anchor attacks or defenses. |
| Shielded Assault | Orion-style assault machines with significant shield protection. |
| Superheavy Assault | The largest battlefield platforms, often treated as strategic assets. |
